what: Cross-script Procrustes alignment calibration: the measured leave-one-out null on the known Linear A <-> Linear B chart-shared signs, plus the self-alignment identity sanity floor. This is the evidence the aegean.analysis.procrustes module ships to document that distributional embedding alignment recovers NO Linear A -> Linear B correspondence signal at this corpus scale.

protocol.source: Linear A sign embeddings from the bundled lineara corpus (aegean.load('lineara'))
protocol.target: Linear B sign embeddings from DAMOS (aegean.load('damos')) RESTRICTED TO SYLLABOGRAMS
protocol.syllabogram_restriction: full-vocab DAMOS embeddings are intractable for the pure-Python SVD, so each DAMOS sign is cleaned with the Linear B Leiden normalizer (aegean.scripts.linearb.lexicon._norm: strip the combining underdot and the [ ] ? markers, fold subscripts, upper-case) and kept only if it is one of the 74 canonical Linear B syllabograms (signClass == 'syllabogram' in the linearb sign inventory); WORD tokens left with fewer than two syllabograms drop out of the co-occurrence model
protocol.anchors: shared_label_anchors(la, lb): the signs the two scripts share by transliteration value (folded label), the AB 'chart-shared' signs, used as the seed dictionary and as the partial ground truth
protocol.leave_one_out: rank_known_pairs(la, lb, pairs, leave_one_out=True): for each known pair the alignment is relearned with that pair held out of the anchors, then the rank of the true target among all target signs is recorded
protocol.execution: CPU, pure Python, deterministic (verified: two runs give identical ranks and identical identity recovery)

corpus.note: 73 of the 74 canonical Linear B syllabograms are attested inside multi-sign words in DAMOS and enter the embedding vocabulary

leave_one_out_null.interpretation: top-1 recovery is exactly 0.000 (no known pair's true target is its nearest neighbour) and top-5 (0.094) is at chance (0.068); the median rank 26 of 73 is modestly better than the chance median 37 but nowhere near recovery. Distributional embedding alignment recovers no usable Linear A -> Linear B correspondence signal at this corpus scale.

identity_sanity_floor.check: recover_identity(la, anchor_fraction=1.0, seed=1): align the Linear A embedding to ITSELF with every sign anchored; a well-behaved method recovers each sign as its own nearest neighbour
identity_sanity_floor.interpretation: self-alignment recovers 90.1% of signs at rank 1 (95.7% within top-5); all 16 misses are distributionally identical signs whose context vectors are collinear (raw cosine > 0.98, tied alignment score 1.0), chiefly hapax *NNN signs that appear in exactly one shared context and so resolve to a tied sibling rather than to themselves. The machinery works; the cross-script top-1 of 0.000 is a genuine null, not a broken alignment.

conclusion: The instrument functions (self-alignment recovers 90.1% of signs) yet recovers 0.0% of the known Linear A -> Linear B pairs at rank 1: distributional embedding alignment carries no recoverable cross-script correspondence signal at the Aegean corpus scale. The module ships as the honest instrument that measured this null, not as a decipherment aid; every output remains exploratory-labelled.

notes.in_sample_overfitting: without leave-one-out (rank_known_pairs(..., leave_one_out=False)) the known pairs rank near-perfectly, because the alignment is fitted on the very pairs it is scored on; only the held-out numbers above are honest.
notes.reproduction_vs_0_37_0: this re-run reproduces the 0.37.0 parked calibration's decisive invariants exactly: 53 shared pairs, 73 target signs, chance median 37, and top-1 = 0.000. Two figures differ from the 0.37.0 working note: the leave-one-out median is 26.0 here (the note recorded 34), and the self-alignment identity recovery is 0.901 here (the note recorded 1.000). The identity gap is explained above (distributional twins; the theoretical 1.0 holds only on a clean, twin-free space); the median difference does not change the qualitative null (top-1 0.000, top-5 at chance, median far short of recovery). The numbers in this file are the ones the module, docs, and tests are pinned to.
